definition: BOJ normalization is a potentially underpriced exogenous shock to US term premium and global correlation regimes. Each 25bp BOJ hike narrows the Fed-BOJ spread, reducing carry incentive and strengthening JPY. Estimated Japanese selling pressure of $100-300B over 12-18 months could add 30-180bp to US term premium, but the magnitude range is too wide for confident positioning.
evidence: Each BOJ rate hike narrows Fed-BOJ spread reducing carry incentive
Japanese institutions gradually reducing UST exposure since 2022 with limited measurable impact
$450B equity ETF position unlikely to be unwound
Japanese institutional repatriation historically ~30% of model-predicted
JGB-dependent bank balance sheets with ~¥150T exposure
Currency hedging economics dampen flow impact
7-18bp UST term premium impact under gradual normalization (quant_researcher_01)
15-30bp represents upper bound under rapid normalization
~$1.6T in foreign holdings, 10-15% repatriation = 15-30bp term premium
BoJ normalization has been priced and repriced multiple times without predicted disruption materializing
Cross-border CLO AAA demand channel adds secondary vector
open_questions: Is the 6x range between low and high estimates actionable?
Has gradual Japanese UST reduction already absorbed much of the potential shock?
How does BOJ communication under Ueda differ from Kuroda in terms of market impact?
What is the pace and terminal level of BoJ normalization?
How much repatriation will actually occur vs model predictions?
Can domestic political constraints prevent normalization entirely?
Will BoJ normalization proceed gradually enough to avoid capital flow disruption?
What is the cross-border CLO AAA demand sensitivity to yen carry unwind?
